rag_rules: Place range assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in type-level contracts.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes.
Ensure type consistency between helper declarations and expressions.
Do not encode AADL property references as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE clauses follow the pattern: assume "label": expression; guarantee "label": expression; eq name : type = expression; const name : type = value; assign output = expression;
Component type contracts should use visible ports and named constants for bounds.
anti_patterns: Do not reference package-qualified identifiers not present in the model's configured dependencies.
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type annexes unless represented through variables.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about package or property set visibility
Implementation-level bindings and subcomponent paths in type contracts
